name: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard 5.0.0 (2025)
summary: The OWASP ASVS 5.0 catalogue of application security requirements, organized into 17 verification chapters across three levels (L1–L3). Serves as the security coverage oracle — the areas a requirements set must address for a security-relevant application; Data Protection maps to legal-privacy.

leaves.name: V1 Encoding and Sanitization
leaves.description: Output encoding and input sanitization to prevent injection (SQLi, XSS, OS command, XXE) in the correct context.

leaves.name: V2 Validation and Business Logic
leaves.description: Input validation and enforcement of business-logic constraints so flows cannot be abused or skipped.

leaves.name: V3 Web Frontend Security
leaves.description: Browser-side security controls — security headers, CSP, cookie attributes, clickjacking and CSRF defenses.

leaves.name: V4 API and Web Service
leaves.description: Security requirements specific to REST, GraphQL and other web-service APIs.

leaves.name: V5 File Handling
leaves.description: Secure upload, download, storage and processing of files to prevent path traversal and malicious content.

leaves.name: V6 Authentication
leaves.description: Verification of user identity — credentials, MFA, password policy, recovery and anti-brute-force.

leaves.name: V7 Session Management
leaves.description: Secure creation, lifetime, rotation and termination of sessions and session tokens.

leaves.name: V8 Authorization
leaves.description: Enforcement of access-control decisions — least privilege, RBAC/ABAC, and function/data-level authorization.

leaves.name: V9 Self-contained Tokens
leaves.description: Secure use of self-contained tokens such as JWT — signing, validation and claim handling.

leaves.name: V10 OAuth and OIDC
leaves.description: Secure configuration and use of OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect flows.

leaves.name: V11 Cryptography
leaves.description: Correct selection and use of cryptographic algorithms, key management and randomness.

leaves.name: V12 Secure Communication
leaves.description: Protection of data in transit — TLS configuration, certificate validation and secure protocols.

leaves.name: V13 Configuration
leaves.description: Secure build, dependency and runtime configuration — secrets management and hardening.

leaves.name: V14 Data Protection
leaves.description: Protection of sensitive data and PII — classification, caching controls, and privacy-preserving handling.

leaves.name: V15 Secure Coding and Architecture
leaves.description: Secure architecture and coding practices — trust boundaries, secure defaults and defense in depth.

leaves.name: V16 Security Logging and Error Handling
leaves.description: Security event logging, safe error handling and protection of log data.

leaves.name: V17 WebRTC
leaves.description: Security requirements for real-time communication using WebRTC.
